title: Infinity Game Music
description: This mod collects the delay between music playbacks
body:
**
Tired of the oppressive silence while playing Minecraft?**

The game's soundtrack, composed by legends like C418, Lena Raine, and Aaron Cherof, provides one of the most atmospheric experiences in gaming. However, vanilla Minecraft has a major flaw: after a track finishes, the game initiates a "silence timer" that can last anywhere from 10 to 20 minutes. For the vast majority of your playtime, you are left hearing nothing but your own footsteps and mob sounds.

Infinite Music Update is a simple yet essential modification for Minecraft 1.21.11 (Fabric) that solves this issue once and for all. It tweaks the game's sound engine to forcibly minimize the delay between tracks.

How it works: Instead of a random pause of thousands of ticks, the mod sets the delay to just 1 second (20 ticks). As soon as one melody fades out, the next one begins almost immediately. This creates a continuous, seamless musical flow, turning your survival experience into a true cinematic adventure.

Why you need this mod:

    Endless Atmosphere: Perfect for long building sessions or exploration when you want constant background accompaniment.

    Works Everywhere: This is a client-side mod. You can install it and play on any server—the music will play more often just for you, without affecting others or requiring server installation.

    Lightweight: The mod adds no new blocks or items, ensuring zero impact on performance.

Fill your world with music and forget about the silence with Infinite Music Update!
